presets.name: 2 Guitar Rig
presets.info:
Instrument: Les Paul (all pickups)
Playback System: Studio headphones

This is a 2-guitar starting point for anyone wanting to jam with a buddy or control your 2nd guitarists tones. I use this type of set up on tour and it works great. Recommend you pan each of the outputs hard left and right to separate the signals.

Snap 1: Base tone - Cleanish starting tone
Snap 2: LIGHT ODS - OD pedals engaged
Snap 3: GAINZZ - Lots of gain on both
Snap 4: Gain Delay - Gain pedals on with some delay
Snap 5: Clean Slap - Clean tones with slap back echo
Snap 6: OD Delays - Overdrive tones with some delay

presets.name: Abe Linkin
presets.info:
Instrument: RG470 (bridge humbucker)
Playback System: Studio monitors, headphones

Snap 1: Cleanish - Low Jack
Snap 2: Rhythm - High Jack with Scream 808
Snap 3: Solo - High Jack with Scream 808 and Gain boost
Snap 4: Delay - High Jack with Ducked Delay
Snap 5: Phasey - High Jack with Scream 808 and light Script Mod Phase

EXP 2: Volume

presets.name: Badonkulous!
presets.info:
Instrument: 8 (or 7) string guitar with bridge humbucker
Playback System: Studio monitors, FRFR

Snap 1: 8 Str Rtm - 8-string metal rhythm tone
Snap 2: 8 Str Cln - 8-string clean tone
Snap 3: 8 Str Lead - 8-string lead tone
Snap 4: 8 Str LoFi - Lo-fi clean tone
Snap 5: Chainsaw - Filthy chainsaw tone
Snap 6: Ld Wham - Lead tone with whammy
Snap 7: 8 Str Cnch - 8-string crunch tone

EXP 1: Pitch Wham
EXP 2: Volume

presets.name: BAS:4 PRO 4 Pros
presets.info:
Instrument: Yamaha BB734A
Playback System: Studio monitors

Snap 1: CleanPunch - Dynamic, no-frills, clean bass sound that fits almost any context
Snap 2: DirtyPunch - Snap 1 + Teemah drive for an aggressive bite
Snap 3: Slap Scoop - Scooped mids + boosted highs for slap definition
Snap 4: Solo Boost - Slight boost in the mids, boost in overall volume for bass solos
Snap 5: Phat Synth - Octave pedal with almost no dry signal; classic sub-bass synth tone
Snap 6: Wet Synth - Growler pedal for a more expressive synth tone
Snap 7: Funk Env - Growler as a gritty envelope filter for a funky tone
Snap 8: FilthyLead - Ultra-compressed, fuzzed out octave up synth tone for lead lines

Stomp A1: Always-on Parametric EQ mostly serves as a conservative hi/lo cut, but some snapshots have boosts/cuts where appropriate. Use this to shape the preset for your specific bass. Gain for each band is snapshot-enabled
Stomp A2: Regal Bass DI adds a slight sheen and warmth to the tone
Stomp A3: Always-on LA Studio Comp adds a moderate amount of compression
Stomp A4: Teemah! makes for a gritty but punchy overdrive that works in most contexts
Stomp A5: Limiter catches any peaks that would otherwise clip the output
Stomp A7: Mutes Path 2A (DI signal)
Stomp A8: Boctaver set to be mostly just the -1 Oct signal
Stomp A9: Growler adds an envelope filter-like synth layer
Stomp A10: DI Limiter catches any peaks that would otherwise clip the output

EXP 2: Volume (both paths)

presets.name: BAS:AmpegSquared
presets.info:
Instrument: Yamaha BB734A
Playback System: Studio monitors

Snap 1: 50/50 - Even split between Ampeg SVT 50th & B15NF 66
Snap 2: 75/25 SVT - Mostly SVT with a bit of B15
Snap 3: 75/25 B15 - Mostly B15 with a bit of SVT
Snap 4: Dual Grit - Even split, higher gain on both amps

Stomp A7: Optical compression for punchiness
Stomp A8: Parallel overdrive for additional bite
Stomp A9: Toggles the SVT's Ultra Lo on/off
Stomp A10: Toggles the SVT's Ultra Hi on/off

EXP 1: Controls split between SVT (toe) & B15 (heel)
EXP 2: Volume

presets.name: BAS:Djentrified
presets.info:
Instrument: Abasi Larada (boosted treble)
Playback System: Studio monitors

Snap 1: Clank - Swedish Chainsaw applied to high frequencies only
Snap 2: More Mids - Full-bodied distortion tone
Snap 3: Amp Grit - Mandarin Plus 200, higher gain
Snap 4: UltraClean - Lots of headroom & presence

Stomp A8: Always-on compression to tighten up signal
Stomp A9: Toggles DI signal (Path 2A) on/off for extra clarity
Stomp A10: Distorts high frequencies only
Stomp A11: Compresses low frequencies only

EXP 1: Hype parameter for Mandarin Plus 200
EXP 2: Volume (both paths)

presets.name: Brit MegaGuitar
presets.info:
In the 'Bass' category you’ll find the 'Brit MegaBass' model, but this preset utilizes it for electric guitar. Includes an enormous variety of effects and snapshots, going from clean to huge leads, ambient, and modulated sounds. Stomp A mode gives you access to most of the included effects, including drives, fuzz, delays, modulation, pitch, and reverbs.

Instrument: HSS Strat (2 position)
Playback System: Studio monitors, headphones

Snap 1: Clean Dot8 - Clean tone with dotted 8th delay
Snap 2: Drive - Stage 1 drive (Minotaur)
Snap 3: Drive + - Stage 2 drive (Prize Drive)
Snap 4: Lead - Solo/lead tone
Snap 5: Fuzz - ...Fuzz
Snap 6: Mega Mod - Two different types of modulation in series with rhythmic delays
Snap 7: Ambi Clean - Clean tone with big delay and reverb
Snap 8: Ambi Lead - Lead tone with big delay and reverb (play melodic lead lines)

presets.name: Callisto Chatter
presets.info:
Tip: Use Poly Sustain to create a drone and then Big Lead to play over it.

Instrument: Strat or Tele
Playback System: Studio monitors

Snap 1: No glitch – Turns off the Glitch delay
Snap 2: Big Lead – This is your "normal" tone
Snap 3: Pitch – This is the everything on tone
Snap 4: No pitch – Turns off the octave up

presets.name: Cine-Plexi
presets.info:
Instrument: Tele, Les Paul, or Strat
Playback System: Studio headphones

This is a "head start" preset for the Brit Plexi fans. Use it as a base and build your own rig!

Snap 1: Clean? - Cleanish starting tone
Snap 2: Dirtier - More gain
Snap 3: THATCrunch - Super crunchy sound
Snap 4: Sauced - Maxed out amp

presets.name: Deconstructed Bliss
presets.info:
This is meant to give you elements commonly found in pedals from a popular Minnesota-based boutique pedal maker, in a modular form.

Instrument: Serus H2 (Pure P.A.F. HB bridge pickup)
Playback System: Studio monitors

Snap 1: Lost Gen - Auto-voume and insane texture. Play slow!
Snap 2: Ambi Cln - Your base tone with verb
Snap 3: Sci-fi Doc - Glitchy and pitchy
Snap 4: Hot Vinyl - Your vinyl has warped
Snap 5: No Feet - Auto-volume does the swells for you

EXP 2: Volume

presets.name: Deluxe Gig
presets.info:
This one has everything you would need on a typical cover gig. Use pedalboard mode and make some cool snapshots!

Instrument: Les Paul (all pickups), Tele
Playback System: Studio headphones

Snap 1: Cleanest - Uh, most clean one!
Snap 2: EoB - Because "Edge of Breakup" did not fit
Snap 3: Crunch - Rock rhythm stuff
Snap 4: On 10! - I put all the knobs to 10
Snap 5: Ambient - Fun textures for some light picking or swells
Snap 6: Trem - Some classic tremolo
Snap 7: Lo Resdryv - Nice drive sound with some Lo Res delay

presets.name: Double Double
presets.info:
This preset utilizes two instances of the US Double Black in a stereo configuration, one set to the Normal input and the other to the Vibrato input. All the modulation, delays, and reverbs are placed after the amp/cab blocks. These amp models are big and clean, but sound great when pushed hard with drives. Spring reverb and Optical tremolo are included to simulate playing through the real amps.

Clip Instrument: Tele (Bridge position)
Playback System: Studio monitors, headphones

Snap 1: Push - Pushed clean tone (Minotaur)
Snap 2: Clean Slap - Clean tone with slap delay
Snap 3: Big Rhythm - Stage 2 drive (Prize Drive)
Snap 4: Lead - Solo/lead tone
Snap 5: Trem Clean - Clean tone with Opto Tremolo
Snap 6: Ambi Vbrto - Clean tone with vibrato and big delay/verb
Snap 7: Rotary Clean - Clean tone rotary
Snap 8: Dot8th Rtm - Rhythm tone with dotted 8th delay

presets.name: Dreambient

Snap 2: Vint Dly - Ambient vintage delay and hall reverb
Snap 3: Tremolo - Tremolo with Hall reverb
Snap 4: Obi Wah - Obi wah with Glitz reverb
Snap 5: Dry - Dry compressed clean, no ambient effects
Snap 6: Chorus - Lush chorus with hall reverb
Snap 7: Loads - Extremely ambient tone

EXP 1: Wah
EXP 2: Volume

presets.name: Ichor
presets.info:
This preset is created for a mixture of different lead sounds.

Instrument: C1-EX with Colossus bridge pickup
Tuning: DADGAD
Type: Lead

Snap 1: Balanced - Slow chorus, fat and wide, good for octave parts under choruses that get out of the way of vocals
Snap 2: Dark - Darkened lead sound for sections where the lead should not be in the forefront
Snap 3: Bright - Guitar solos at the forefront, ripping
Snap 4: Flash - Harmonized for D minor, reminded me of Flash Gordon harmonies
Snap 5: Low Spank - Lower gain, great with split coils for spank
Snap 6: Low Dry - No effects on the Low Spank sound
Snap 7: Dry Gain - High gain, dry

presets.name: Jazzy Jazz
presets.info:

Snap 3: Spacey - Clarity Amp, light reverb, and delay (split dynamically to only let certain frequencies through to the delay)
Snap 4: Oct Down - Clarity Amp, light reverb, and Simple Pitch for octave down

EXP 2: Volume

presets.info:

Instrument: Serus H2 (Pure P.A.F. HB bridge pickup)

Snap 2: Wet Split - Reverb to the left, delay to the right
Snap 3: Lite OD - Heir Apparent on
Snap 4: Crunch - Hedgehog on
Snap 5: Lead - Heir pushing the Hedgehog
Snap 6: Slap Cln - Forward slap delay only
Snap 7: Chaos - Hedgehog, Alpaca, harm trem, and wet effects bring the (controlled) chaos

EXP 2: Volume

presets.name: Nash Sesh
presets.info:
Instrument: Tele or Les Paul (all pickups)
Playback System: Studio headphones

This is a "do it all" recording session board inspired by the players on the scene in Nashville. Definitely a more modern country flare, but the classic stuff is there too!

Snap 1: Dluxe Base - Cleanish starting tone with slap
Snap 2: Ambient - The "vibey intro"
Snap 3: Trem - The "vibey verse"
Snap 4: Dlx Solo - Classic Rock-type solo
Snap 5: Edgelay - Rhythmic delay they all love
Snap 6: FuzzSolo - Fuzzy solo with slapback
Snap 7: Dry Power - Realistically, you will be on this one most
Snap 8: BrghtSolo - Bright solo to cut through and let it rip

presets.info:
Instrument: Highway One Tele w/ SD Noiseless P90s (bridge pickup)
Playback System: Studio monitors, headphones

Snap 2: Crunch - Ratatouille with light overdrive
Snap 3: Prize - Prize Drive with light overdrive
Snap 4: Crunchier - Ratatouille and Prize Drive together for some good gain
Snap 5: Delay Funs - Prize Drive with Transistor Tape Delay 1/8 with long feedback
Snap 6: Harm Trem - Clean with Harmonic Tremolo (almost like a univibe)
Snap 7: Leslie - Rotary set to slow
Snap 8: Slap - Clean with Tape Echo slapback delay

EXP 2: Volume

presets.name: Reflection
presets.info:
This preset uses Double Take to sound like more than one guitar player. Use of fuzz and sub-octave effects here as well. Noise gate between amp and cab is sidechained from the input.

Instrument: C1-EX with Colossus bridge pickup
Tuning: DADGAD
Type: Rhythm/Destroy

Snap 1: Two of Me - Same amp and cab but with different mics on the cabs between the two sides to make it larger 
Snap 2: No Boost - Same as above but without Graphic EQ and boost
Snap 3: Fuzz - Added fuzz, works best with single notes
Snap 4: Power - Added -5 pitch so every note sounds like a power chord with fuzz
Snap 5: End Them - Same as Power with pitch at -12, added boost
Snap 6: End Me - Pitch at -12 with no Graphic EQ or boost, works great with single notes
Snap 7: Power Dist - Everything on but back to the -5 pitch, works well with single notes

presets.name: Reigning Champ
presets.info:
Instrument: Strat or Tele
Playback System: Studio monitors

Snap 1: Rhythm – Bone dry Champ

presets.name: Rhythm Concepts
presets.info:
This preset was created as a concept, to let you hear the subtle (but in a mix, massive) differences between fine tuning a guitar sound for 6-7-8 string tunings using the same amp. 

Most amplifier builders have fine-tuned their amps using Standard Guitar Tuning. When we approach with lower tuned instruments we need to dial the frequencies closer to what the input of an amp is expecting. We’re after the 'timbre' of lower tuned instruments - not the mud.

For ease of use, I used the Poly Capo so you can hear the differences when using one guitar. I used a guitar in DADGAD tuning; clear Poly Capo to hear the differences in your different tunings. 

Instrument: C1-EX with Colossus bridge pickup
Type: Rhythm

Snap 1: D Tuning
Snap 2: C# Tuning
Snap 3: C Tuning - Pre-EQ added as we start getting ‘mud’ around the 100-250hz region, gain lowered slightly on amp
Snap 4: Bb Tuning - 100-250hz cut a bit more, gain added to Pre-EQ, a bit less gain, EQ moves with amp for more 'cut.' We start staging the gain from this tuning onward, adding more volume from the pedals and reducing the gain on the amp for more clarity and transient attack
Snap 5: Ab Tuning - Same as Bb tuning with some subtle changes
Snap 6: F Tuning - Tuned for 8-string guitars
Snap 7: Eb Tuning - Lower 8-string tuning
Snap 8: Octaves - Blended in lower octave for filth

presets.name: Unlikely Pair
presets.info:
This preset utilizes two amps that might not be typically paired together: US Luxe Black and Solid 100. US Luxe Black handles the clean tones while Solid 100 handles the rhythm and lead tones. Stomp A9 toggles the two amps manually.

Instrument: DGT semi-hollow (bridge pickup)
Playback System: Studio monitors, headphones

Snap 1: Mod Drive - Rhythm tone with modulation (80's rhythm inspired)
Snap 2: Slo Rhythm - Solid 100 amp with minimal effects
Snap 3: Dot8thPush - Pushed rhythm tone with dotted 8th delay
Snap 4: Solo - Solo/lead tone
Snap 5: Clean Dlx - Clean tone with US Luxe Black amp
Snap 6: Big Verb - Ambient cleans
Snap 7: Retro Reel - Clean tone with Retro Reel modulation
Snap 8: ShimrSwell - Big ambient swells with shimmer

EXP Toe: Turns Pitch Wham on/off
EXP 1: Pitch Wham Position and Mix
